{{Quote|It isn't generally known, Luke, but there's some kind of leak in the Council. Information's getting out to Admiral Pellaeon and to the Imperial Moffs like Getelles and Shargael over in I-sector. Minister of State Rieekan thinks it may be through someone in the Rationalist Party—maybe even Q-Varx himself, though I think the man's honest. They have adherents both in the Republic and in nearly every piece of the Empire still big enough to field a fleet.|Leia Organa Solo to Luke Skywalker|<ref name="PoT">''[[Planet of Twilight]]''</ref>}}
'''Bliss Shargael''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]]. With the disintegration of the Empire following [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] death at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], in the leaderless vacuum, the title of “[[Warlord of the Empire|Warlord]]” was hijacked by power-hungry Imperials of nonexistent integrity. Undeserving small fish like [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Inos Fonada]] and Moff [[Walang Grazz]] and second-tier [[replacement warlord]]s like Shargael himself and fellow replacement warlord [[Jmanuel Tethys]] poached the rank in all it's supercilious glory.<ref name="DoaE1">{{SW|url=news/the-imperial-warlords-despoilers-of-an-empire-part-1|series=The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref> He also governed the [[Illisurevimurasi sector/Legends|I-sector]].

His sector was presumably absorbed by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], during the Borderlands Campaign against the [[Imperial Remnant]] for their involvement in the [[Death Seed]] Plague of [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]].
